name: Jack Walrath
abstract: Jack Arthur Walrath (born May 5, 1946) is an American post-bop jazz trumpeter and musical arranger known for his work with Ray Charles, Gary Peacock, Charles Mingus, and Glenn Ferris, among others.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Newport Jazz Festival: Sam Rivers Quartet and Orchestra
Kool Jazz Festival: A Night of Genius-Artists Celebrate Mingus
JVC Jazz Festival: An Evening of Music by Paul Nash

about_quotes.evidence: whether it was Dizzy Reese or whether it was Jack Walrath or whether it was, I mean, I can’t even start to name the names, all kinds of young contemporary musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Sandke
about_quotes.evidence: to quote Jack Walrath, who was kind of like saying, he was like speaking against this idea of trying to form, saying that this is jazz, this isn't jazz
